evals.prompt: I'm building a settings page for a Next.js app using shadcn/ui. I need to add a notification preferences section where users can toggle email, SMS, and push notification alerts on or off. Build the React component for this section.
evals.expected_output: A composed component using existing shadcn/ui primitives (Switch/Card/form controls), semantic color tokens, flex+gap spacing, Base UI APIs (render prop not asChild), and a demonstration of the reusability ladder — checking existing components before writing custom code.
expectations: Uses semantic color tokens (bg-primary, text-muted-foreground etc.) — no raw Tailwind palette values like text-gray-500 or bg-white
Uses flex + gap-* for spacing — no space-y-* or space-x-*
Composes with existing shadcn/ui components (e.g. Card, Switch, Label) rather than building from scratch with raw divs
Does not use asChild — uses render prop pattern for Base UI or omits it correctly
className props are used for layout only (max-w, mx-auto, mt-4 etc.), not color/typography overrides

evals.expected_output: Refactored component that: replaces space-y-*/space-x-* with flex+gap-*, replaces w-8 h-8 with size-8, removes raw palette colors (text-gray-*, bg-green-*, bg-red-*, bg-white), uses semantic tokens or Badge component for status, and uses cn() for any conditional classes.
expectations: Removes space-y-4, space-y-1, space-x-3, space-x-2 — replaced with flex + gap-* equivalents
Removes raw palette colors: text-gray-900, text-gray-500, bg-green-100, text-green-800, bg-red-100, text-red-800, bg-white
Uses semantic tokens for text and background (e.g. text-foreground, text-muted-foreground, bg-card)
Status indicator uses Badge component or semantic tokens, not raw color spans
Conditional className uses cn() not a ternary string inside className

evals.prompt: Our Tailwind v4 + shadcn/ui design system needs a 'success' state. I want to be able to use bg-success and text-success-foreground as Tailwind utility classes in my components. Walk me through how to add this.
evals.expected_output: Step-by-step: (1) define --success and --success-foreground in :root in globals.css using OKLCH format, (2) register them in the @theme inline block as --color-success and --color-success-foreground, (3) usage examples. Should also note to edit tailwindCssFile from project context, not create a new file.
expectations: Defines CSS variables in :root using OKLCH format — not HSL or hex
Adds --color-success: var(--success) and --color-success-foreground: var(--success-foreground) in the @theme inline block
Instructs to edit the existing global CSS file (tailwindCssFile), not create a new one
Shows a usage example with bg-success and text-success-foreground in JSX

evals.prompt: I'm working on a SolidJS app with shadcn-solid. I need a component that shows a notification count badge — green when the count is normal, red when it's over 99. Build this for me.
evals.expected_output: Solid mode: uses class (not className), checks for existing Badge component first, extends with a cva variant only if reused (not a one-off), uses semantic color tokens (not raw greens/reds), and uses For/Index for lists rather than .map(). Should demonstrate the reusability ladder.
expectations: Uses class attribute instead of className (Solid mode)
Considers or uses the existing Badge component rather than building a custom div from scratch
Uses semantic color tokens for the green/red states — not raw values like text-green-600 or bg-red-500
Does not use asChild — uses Kobalte/Base UI render pattern or composes correctly for Solid
